sections.heading: Content
sections.content:
Differentiation is a branch of calculus that deals with the rates of change.
CALCULUS is a branch of Mathematics that deals with continuously varying quantities.

sections.heading: FIRST PRINCIPLE
sections.content:
This is the method of finding the derivative of a function from definition.
EXAMPLE
Using first principle, differentiate the function y =
SOLUTION
Y = + x – 1,
Let y = and x = (substitute into the equation)
= ( + -1
= ( + 2x + + + x – 1 …..(i)
Subtract the original equation from (i)
= (+ 2x + x -1 – (+ x -1)
= ( + 2x + (divide through by
= + 2x + 1 i.e.
= 0 +2x + 1 = 2x + 1

sections.heading: STANDARD DERIVATIVE
sections.content:
This is the general rule for derivative function. i.e.
= na, where n is the power of x, a is the coefficient of x.
NOTE: when differentiating any function the constant value becomes zero since the derivative is with respect to x.
EXAMPLE
Differentiate 3 - 7
SOLUTION
= (3X4- (7x3)+ (1x2)+ (7x1) – 0
= 12- 21 + 2x +7
